name: rEstus
description: Variant of ASAP3 optimized for non-excitable cells, shifting voltage sensitivity to match their characteristically negative resting potentials.

spectrum.name: rEstus
spectrum.note: Excitation (dashed; includes the ~400 nm neutral-form shoulder) and emission digitized from Fig. S4 of the rEstus2s paper — rEstus measured in HEK293T cells; anionic excitation peak ~463 nm, emission ~513 nm.

voltage.name: rEstus
voltage.note: ΔF/F points read directly from the mKate2-rEstus brightness–voltage curve in Fig. 1G, which plots molecular brightness B(%EGFP) vs Vm with the fit passing through the mean data points. Converted to ΔF/F relative to −70 mV via ΔF/F = (B(V) − B(−70))/B(−70) (B ∝ FcpGFP). Independently re-digitized from the Fig. 1G teal data points during audit — values agree with the stored curve within ≤2%; monotonic negative-going, −70 mV normalized to 0, −70→+30 mV chord ≈ −61% (consistent with dynamicRangeData −62%). Consistent with the Table 1 Boltzmann fit (Bmax 77.5, ΔBmax 57.0, Vhalf −32.0, ks 26.0 %EGFP).

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean of double-exponential fits read from Fig. S9 (HEK293T, photodiode, 23°C, n=5). Activation (-80->+40 mV step, read at +40 mV): tau_fast ~2.5 ms, tau_slow ~28 ms, A_fast 57.0%. Deactivation (repolarizing step to -100 mV): tau_fast ~33 ms, tau_slow ~72 ms, A_fast 53%. Weighted tau_on = 0.57*2.5 + 0.43*28 = 13.5 ms; tau_off = 0.53*33 + 0.47*72 = 51 ms. The paper notes the response to repolarizing steps is slower for rEstus than ASAP3, consistent with the large tau_off.

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F for a true 100-mV step (-70 -> +30 mV) computed from the Fig. 1G / Table 1 brightness-voltage Boltzmann fit (Bmax=77.5, ΔBmax=57.0, Vhalf=-32.0, ks=26.0 %EGFP): B(-70)=66.8, B(+30)=25.3 -> -62%. One-photon widefield (470 nm LED) voltage clamp, HEK293T cells. The full-span -70 -> +60 mV change is -67%.

subthresholdData.note: Peak fractional voltage sensitivity (dF/F dV-1, %/mV) read from Fig. S2 of the rEstus2s paper, the first derivative of the Boltzmann F-V fit divided by the fit; rEstus (black) peaks at ~1.25 %/mV near -10 to 0 mV. Re-sourced here because the origin paper's Fig. 1H plots absolute |dB/dVm| in %EGFP/mV (a different quantity), not fractional %/mV. On the same axis the sibling rEstus2s (red) peaks at ~2.0 %/mV near +5 mV (about 1.6-fold over rEstus across the -100 to 0 mV resting range).

brightnessData.note: Ratio of maximum molecular brightness (Bmax, %EGFP at saturating negative Vm) from the origin paper Table 1 / Fig. 1G: mKate2-rEstus Bmax = 77.5 ± 1.7 %EGFP vs mKate2-ASAP3 Bmax = 37.5 ± 2.2 %EGFP -> 77.5/37.5 = 2.07. Same-paper Bmax comparison; distinct from the Nair et al. FLIM entry below (rEstus/ASAP3 = 8.49/4.96 = 1.71 Fgreen/Fred), which is a different brightness measurement (relative to mKate2 at -120 mV) in a different paper.

Relative molecular brightness (Fgreen/Fred at -120 mV) cross-measured against ASAP3 in the Nair et al. FLIM paper. Ratio of the fitted Max Fgreen/Fred parameters (Eq. 1 fits, Table S1): rEstus 8.49 ± 0.04 / ASAP3 4.96 ± 0.02 = 1.71. This uses the same convention as the sibling cross-entries in the repo (JEDI-1P/ASAP3 = 8.48/4.96 = 1.71; ASAP1/ASAP3 = 4.66/4.96 = 0.94). Evaluating the full Eq. 1 fits at exactly -120 mV (including the Offset floor) gives rEstus 7.94 / ASAP3 4.06 = 1.96, but the bare Max-parameter ratio is the consistent cross-comparison quantity.

researchPapers.title: An Ultrasensitive Genetically Encoded Voltage Indicator Uncovers the Electrical Activity of Non-Excitable Cells
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells; A375 melanoma cells; MCF-7 breast cancer cells, in vitro

researchPapers.title: Sub-Millivolt Voltage Imaging Reveals Gap Junction-Mediated Bioelectric Contact Inhibition
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells, in vitro (rEstus characterized under whole-cell voltage clamp alongside rEstus2s)

researchPapers.title: Absolute Membrane Potential Recording with ASAP-Type Genetically Encoded Voltage Indicators Using Fluorescence Lifetime Imaging
researchPapers.sample: HEK293T cells; A375 melanoma cells; MCF-7 breast cancer cells, in vitro

researchPapers.title: Crosstalk of KCNH1 and KCNH5 gain-of-function mutations leading to epilepsy and neurodevelopmental disorders
Generation and characterization of a human induced pluripotent stem cell line (hiPSC) expressing the rEstus voltage sensor under doxycycline induction
